pattern: Counting how many times each value occurs in one array, then walking the other array and emitting a value while its remaining count is positive, yields each shared element exactly as many times as it appears in both.
hints: An element belongs in the result as many times as it appears in both arrays at once, so plain set membership is not enough - the multiplicity matters.
Tally the occurrences of every value in the smaller array, then scan the larger one, decrementing a tally each time you take a value.
Build a frequency map of one array; for each value in the other array, if its remaining count is above zero, append it and decrease that count by one.

edge_inputs.description: duplicates shared, result keeps the matching multiplicity
values shared once each despite repeats in the second array
no common element yields an empty intersection
shared value appears more often in one array, capped by the smaller count
zero is a valid value and must be matched
fully identical arrays return all elements

stress.description: two large arrays drawn from a small value range so overlaps with varied multiplicities occur
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    a = [rng.randint(0, 40) for _ in range(n)]
    b = [rng.randint(0, 40) for _ in range(rng.randint(1, n))]
    return [a, b]
stress.note: narrow value range forces frequent multiset overlaps
